title: Reading and Use of English – Part 1
description: For questions 1–8, read the text below and choose which answer (A, B, C or D) best completes each gap.

content.text: Regular exercise is widely (0) as one of the best things you can do for your health. Physical activity helps maintain a healthy weight, strengthens muscles, and reduces the (1) of many chronic diseases. Studies have shown that exercise can also have significant benefits for mental health, helping to (2) stress and improve mood. Despite knowing these facts, many people still (3) to incorporate enough physical activity into their daily lives. Modern lifestyles, with long working hours and sedentary jobs, make it challenging to find time for exercise. However, experts suggest that even small (4) of activity throughout the day can make a difference. Taking the stairs instead of the lift or going for a short walk during lunch breaks are simple ways to stay more active. The key is to find activities you enjoy, as this makes it easier to (5) to a regular exercise routine. Group activities and sports can provide additional (6) through social interaction. It's never too late to start exercising, and the health (7) can be seen at any age. Making exercise a (8) should be a priority for everyone who wants to live a longer, healthier life.

example.explanation: 'Recognised as' is the correct expression to indicate that something is widely accepted as true.

questions.explanation: 'Reduce the risk' is the standard placement in healthcare contexts.
'Stick to' is the correct phrasal verb that means to stay true to something.
'Fail to' significa no lograr hacer algo, expresando incapacidad o negligencia.
'Amounts of activity' is the correct expression to refer to amounts of physical activity.
'Stick to' is the correct phrasal verb that means to stay true to something.
'Motivation' is the most appropriate noun to describe the extra boost that group activities provide.
'Health benefits' is the standard collocation for health benefits.
'Make something a habit' is a fixed expression that means to make something a habit.
